prerequisites.1_environment: Ensure JWT_SECRET is set in .env file

socket_client_setup.before:
Old way (no auth):
const socket = io('http://localhost:5000');
socket_client_setup.after:
New way (with JWT):
const socket = io('http://localhost:5000', { auth: { token: 'your_jwt_token' } });
socket_client_setup.important: Without valid token, socket connection will be rejected with 'No token provided' error

scenario_1_socket_auth.name: Test JWT Authentication on Socket Connect
steps: 1. Try connecting without token -> Should fail with 'No token provided'
2. Try connecting with invalid token -> Should fail with 'Invalid token'
3. Try connecting with valid token -> Should succeed and log '[Socket] Token verified for user'

scenario_2_room_based_messaging.name: Test Room-Based Communication (Not Global Broadcast)
steps: 1. Open 3 browser windows
2. Window 1: Provider (provider123) connects with token
3. Window 2: Customer (customer456) connects with token
4. Window 3: Another Provider (provider789) connects with token
5. Provider123 and Customer456 join same chat room via 'join-chat' event
6. Provider123 sends message to Customer456
7. Verify: Provider789 (in Window 3) DOES NOT receive the message
8. Verify: Only Provider123 and Customer456 receive it
scenario_2_room_based_messaging.expected: Messages are room-specific, not broadcast to all users

scenario_3_typing_indicator.name: Test Typing Indicator (Room-Only, Not Self)
steps: 1. Two users in same chat room
2. User A starts typing
3. User A should NOT see their own 'user-typing' event
4. User B should see User A typing
5. User A stops typing
6. User B should see 'user-stop-typing' event

scenario_4_message_persistence.name: Test Message Saved to Database
steps: 1. Send message via socket
2. Check MongoDB Chat collection
3. Message should exist with: chatId, senderId, status='delivered'
4. Call GET /api/chat/messages/:chatId to retrieve
5. Message should appear in results

scenario_5_delete_ownership.name: Test Delete Only Works for Sender
steps: 1. User A sends message
2. User A tries DELETE /api/chat/messages/:messageId -> Should succeed (200)
3. Send another message from User A
4. User B tries DELETE same message -> Should fail (403 Forbidden)
5. Error message should be 'Only sender can delete message'

scenario_6_read_ownership.name: Test Read Only Works for Receiver
steps: 1. User A sends message to User B
2. User B tries PUT /api/chat/messages/:messageId/read -> Should succeed (200)
3. Message status should change to 'read'
4. User A tries to mark same message as read -> Should fail (403)
5. Error should be 'Only receiver can mark message as read'

scenario_7_chat_access.name: Test User Can Only Access Their Own Chats
steps: 1. User A and User B chat
2. User C tries GET /api/chat/messages/userA_userB -> Should fail (403)
3. Error: 'You don't have access to this chat'
4. User A calls same endpoint -> Should succeed (200)

scenario_8_analytics_customer.name: Test Analytics: Customer Providers Count
steps: 1. Customer sends messages to 3 different providers
2. Call GET /api/chat/stats/customer/providers-count with customer JWT
3. Response should be { totalProvidersChatted: 3 }
4. Add message from 4th provider
5. Call again -> Should return 4

scenario_9_analytics_provider.name: Test Analytics: Provider Customers Count
steps: 1. Provider receives messages from 5 different customers
2. Call GET /api/chat/stats/provider/customers-count with provider JWT
3. Response should be { totalCustomersChatted: 5 }

scenario_10_unread_count.name: Test Unread Message Count
steps: 1. User B receives 5 unread messages
2. Call GET /api/chat/unread/:userId with User B JWT -> Should return 5
3. Mark 2 messages as read
4. Call again -> Should return 3

scenario_11_mark_chat_read.name: Test Mark Entire Chat as Read
steps: 1. Chat has 10 unread messages for User B
2. Call PUT /api/chat/chat/:chatId/read-all with User B JWT
3. All 10 messages should be marked as read
4. GET /api/chat/unread/:userId should return 0 for that chat

postman_testing.2_variables: Set {{jwt_token}} variable with valid token from your login endpoint
4_test_flow: 3. Get messages endpoint (should show new messages)

database_verification.check_messages: db.chats.findOne() -> Should have new fields: status, readAt, deliveredAt

console_logging.socket_logs: Look for [Socket] prefix - shows socket events
console_logging.service_logs: Look for [Chat Service] prefix - shows DB operations
console_logging.api_logs: Look for [Chat API] prefix - shows REST API calls
console_logging.example: [Socket] User joined chat room: { userId, chatRoomId, socketId }

error_no_token.fix: Provide token in socket auth header

error_403_forbidden.cause: User trying to access/modify message they don't own
error_403_forbidden.fix: Only sender can delete, only receiver can mark read

error_user_not_in_chat.cause: User not part of chat room trying to access it
error_user_not_in_chat.fix: Only users in chatId can access messages
